name: Closed Guard Hip Bump Sweep to Mount Armbar
stabilization_checkpoints: Secure mount before attacking — grapevines or heel hooks on hips
transition_checkpoints: Hip bump mechanic: sit up, post hand, sweep over shoulder
submission_checkpoints: Isolate arm → figure-four grip → hips high → lean back to extend

failure_detail.mount_lost: Falls to half guard or guard — positions already taught
failure_detail.armbar_fails: Opponent stacks — return to mount or closed guard

duality_links.defense: posture in guard, mount escape, armbar defense

name: Open Guard Scissor Sweep to Mount
control_checkpoints: Collar grip + sleeve grip, shin across belly
stabilization_checkpoints: Follow the sweep — post immediately on landing in mount
transition_checkpoints: Shin across belly → pull collar down → kick bottom leg → roll over

failure_detail.sweep_fails: Still in open guard (safe — retain grips)

name: Closed Guard Flower Sweep to Mount
control_checkpoints: Deep collar grip + same-side sleeve grip
transition_checkpoints: Pull collar → kick far leg over → roll into mount

name: Closed Guard Arm Drag to Back Control RNC
stabilization_checkpoints: Secure seatbelt grip before attacking choke
transition_checkpoints: Arm drag: 2-on-1, pull arm across, hip out, climb behind
submission_checkpoints: Clear collar defense → slide choking arm under chin → lock figure-four → squeeze

failure_detail.back_lost: Opponent turns in — land in mount or closed guard (safe)
failure_detail.rnc_fails: Opponent defends chin — maintain back control, re-attack

duality_links.defense: posture in guard, back escape, RNC defense

name: Butterfly Sweep to Mount
control_checkpoints: Double underhooks seated, head on chest side
stabilization_checkpoints: Land in mount, immediately establish base
transition_checkpoints: Off-balance to one side → hook lift → roll over → land in mount

failure_detail.sweep_fails: Still seated in butterfly (safe — re-grip, re-try)

name: Side Control to Mount Progression
control_checkpoints: Crossface + chest pressure in side control
stabilization_checkpoints: Each position is stable — can pause and consolidate
transition_checkpoints: Side → KOB: slide knee onto belly. KOB → Mount: swing far leg over

failure_detail.mount_lost: Falls to half guard (safe — already taught)

duality_links.defense: side escape, KOB escape, mount escape

name: Closed Guard Triangle Choke
transition_checkpoints: Push one arm across → hips up → leg over far shoulder → lock ankles
submission_checkpoints: Cut angle → pull head down → squeeze knees

failure_detail.triangle_fails: Opponent postures out of half-locked triangle — return to guard
failure_detail.stacking: Opponent stacks — can unlock and return to guard, or sweep to mount

name: Mount Cross Collar Choke
control_checkpoints: High mount — walk knees to armpits
stabilization_checkpoints: Secure first grip deep in collar before attacking
submission_checkpoints: First hand deep in cross collar → second hand in → squeeze elbows together

name: Collar-Sleeve Guard Pull to Closed Guard
control_checkpoints: Collar grip four fingers deep, sleeve grip at wrist — establish before pulling
transition_checkpoints: Foot on hip to control distance, pull collar down, swing hips under, lock feet behind back

failure_detail.pull_fails: Still in open guard (safe — retain grips, try again)
failure_detail.guard_not_closed: Feet don't lock — becomes open guard, not worse

duality_links.defense: guard pull defense, staying standing, posture

name: Turtle to Back Take
stabilization_checkpoints: Seatbelt first → then insert hooks one at a time
transition_checkpoints: Seatbelt grip → hip to mat → insert bottom hook → top hook
submission_checkpoints: Hand fight to clear collar grip → slide arm under chin → lock RNC

failure_detail.back_take_fails: Opponent sits out — scramble to side control or re-engage turtle (recoverable)
failure_detail.hooks_lost: Opponent rolls — can land in mount if hooks are lost (safe)

name: Open Guard Pass to Side Control
stabilization_checkpoints: Clear legs completely before settling side control
transition_checkpoints: Grip ankles → push legs to one side → pass to opposite side → settle

failure_detail.pass_fails: Opponent re-guards — still in open guard (safe)

name: Half Guard Pass to Side Control Americana
control_checkpoints: Crossface + chest pressure in side control
stabilization_checkpoints: Flatten opponent before passing — deny underhook
transition_checkpoints: Knee slice: free trapped leg, slide knee across, clear to side control
submission_checkpoints: Pin wrist to mat → figure-four grip → paint brush away from head

failure_detail.side_lost: Opponent re-guards — back to half guard (safe)
failure_detail.americana_fails: Opponent straightens arm — transition to armbar or maintain control

duality_links.defense: half guard retention, side escape, americana defense

name: Kimura Sweep from Closed Guard
stabilization_checkpoints: Secure kimura grip — figure-four is locked before sweeping
transition_checkpoints: Hip out to kimura side → use grip leverage to sweep → land on top

failure_detail.sweep_fails: Retain guard with kimura grip — can transition to armbar or triangle

reusable_mechanics: kimura grip (reusable — appears in half guard, side control)

name: Mount Attack Cycle: Choke-Armbar Dilemma
transition_checkpoints: Read opponent's arm defense — neck or arms?
submission_checkpoints: Choke: elbows open. Armbar: arms tight. Americana: arm pinned

failure_detail.bridged_off: Land in guard (safe — taught in S1/S7)

name: Back Control Attack System
control_checkpoints: Seatbelt grip, both hooks in, chest glued to back
stabilization_checkpoints: Maintain hooks when opponent tries to escape
transition_checkpoints: Hand fight to clear collar defense
submission_checkpoints: RNC if chin exposed. Bow and arrow if lapel available. Armbar if arm isolatable

failure_detail.opponent_escapes: Opponent turns in → land in mount (safe — already best position)
failure_detail.hooks_lost: Transition to body triangle or re-insert

name: Open Guard to Standing Takedown to Dominant
control_checkpoints: Feet on hips, collar/sleeve or 2-on-1 grip
transition_checkpoints: Technical standup → re-engage with takedown or pull guard

failure_detail.standup_fails: Opponent follows down — still in open guard (safe)

name: Half Guard Underhook Sweep to Mount
control_checkpoints: Win underhook — get to hip, head under chin
transition_checkpoints: Come to knees → drive forward → sweep over trapped leg → land in mount

failure_detail.underhook_lost: Still in half guard bottom (safe — re-pummel)
failure_detail.sweep_fails: Dogfight position — scramble back to half guard or re-attack

name: Half Guard to Side Control to Back Control
transition_checkpoints: Pass half → settle side control → opponent turns → slide hooks in

failure_detail.back_lost: Opponent turns in — land in mount or side control (safe)

duality_links.defense: HG retention, side escape, back escape

name: Side Control Arm Triangle
control_checkpoints: Heavy crossface — opponent's chin forced to far side
stabilization_checkpoints: Lock figure-four around head+arm before moving
transition_checkpoints: Walk around to opposite side or hop to mount → squeeze
submission_checkpoints: Gable grip → chest to floor → squeeze elbows

failure_detail.choke_fails: Maintain side control — re-attack or transition

name: Closed Guard to Side Control Sweep
transition_checkpoints: Overhook + far leg → bridge and roll → land in side control

name: Closed Guard Attack Tree: Sweep-or-Submit
control_checkpoints: Posture break is the root — everything flows from here
transition_checkpoints: Decision tree: read opponent's reaction, choose branch
submission_checkpoints: Triangle or kimura from failed sweep defense

failure_detail.all_fail: Still in closed guard (safe — the safest position to fail from)

name: Half Guard Passing Network
control_checkpoints: Win the underhook battle — this determines everything
transition_checkpoints: Read opponent's frame → choose pass → clear leg → settle

failure_detail.pass_fails: Still in half guard top (safe — dominant position)
failure_detail.swept: Guard player sweeps — land in half guard bottom (recoverable)

name: Positional Ladder: Guard → Side → Mount → Back → Finish
control_checkpoints: Every position is a stable checkpoint — can pause at any step
stabilization_checkpoints: Consolidate each position 3-5 seconds before advancing
transition_checkpoints: Pass → settle → advance → settle → advance → finish

failure_detail.at_any_step: Falling back one step is always safe (mount → side → guard)

reusable_mechanics: All prior mechanics combined — this is the synthesis road

name: Guard Pull to Sweep-or-Submit Chain
stabilization_checkpoints: Close guard immediately — don't settle in open guard if you want closed
transition_checkpoints: Grip → sit → pull into closed guard → immediate posture break

failure_detail.pull_fails: Opponent sprawls — seated in open guard (recoverable)
failure_detail.guard_passed: Opponent passes during pull — land in half guard or side control bottom (risky if unprepared)

duality_links.defense: guard pull defense, pass on the way down
